svg.title: FOUNDATIONS 5 — Great Mosque of Kairouan, North Africa (founded 670 CE)
svg.desc: Local BeHistorical artwork for The World at c.1200 / Thinking Like a Historian. Historical anchor: The Great Mosque of Kairouan in modern Tunisia — one of the oldest mosques in the Islamic world — served simultaneously as prayer hall, legal court, school, and community center. Its architectural scale and permanence communicated Islamic political and religious authority across North Africa. By c.1200, mosques like this one anchored *madrasa* networks, *waqf*-funded institutions, and *qadi* courts across the entire Dar al-Islam..
